name: redwood
description: Redwood provides a first-class CLI that helps you at every stage of development, from your first commit to your first deploy

subcommands.name: dev
subcommands.description: Start development servers for api and web

options.description: String of one or more Webpack Dev Server config options

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploy your redwood project to a hosting provider target

subcommands.name: aws
subcommands.description: Deploy to AWS using the selected provider

options.description: Show help for command

subcommands.name: netlify
subcommands.description: Build command for Netlify deploy

options.description: Build for production [default: "true"]
Apply database migrations [default: "true"]
Migrate the data in your database [default: "true"]

subcommands.name: render
subcommands.description: Build (web) and Start (api) command for Render deploy

options.description: Run server for api in production [default: "true"]
Apply database migrations [default: "true"]
Migrate the data in your database [default: "true"]

subcommands.name: vercel
subcommands.description: Build command for Vercel deploy

options.description: Build for production [default: "true"]
Apply database migrations [default: "true"]
Migrate the data in your database [default: "true"]

subcommands.name: destroy
subcommands.description: Rollback changes made by the generate command

subcommands.name: cell
subcommands.description: Destroy a cell component

subcommands.name: component
subcommands.description: Destroy a component

subcommands.name: function
subcommands.description: Destroy a Function

subcommands.name: layout
subcommands.description: Destroy a layout component

subcommands.name: page
subcommands.description: Destroy a page and route component

args.name: path
args.description: URL path to the page. Defaults to name

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: Destroy pages, SDL, and Services files based on a given DB schema Model

subcommands.name: sdl
subcommands.description: Destroy a GraphQL schema and service component based on a given DB schema Model

subcommands.name: service
subcommands.description: Destroy a service component

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Save time by generating boilerplate code

subcommands.name: cell
subcommands.description: Generate a cell component

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Use this flag to generate a list cell. This flag is needed when dealing with irregular words whose plural and singular is identical such as equipment or pokemon
Generate test files [default: true]
Generate Storybook files [default: true]

subcommands.name: component
subcommands.description: Generate a component component

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Generate test files [default: true]
Generate Storybook files [default: true]

subcommands.name: dataMigration
subcommands.description: Generate a data migration component

subcommands.name: directive
subcommands.description: Generate a directive

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Directive type [Choices: "validator", "transformer"]

subcommands.name: function
subcommands.description: Generate a Function

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.name: layout
subcommands.description: Generate a layout component

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Generate test files [default: true]
Generate Storybook files [default: true]
Generate a layout with a skip link [default: false]

subcommands.name: page
subcommands.description: Generate a page component

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Generate test files [default: true]
Generate Storybook files [default: true]

args.name: path
args.description: URL path to the page. Defaults to name

subcommands.name: scaffold
subcommands.description: Generate Pages, SDL, and Services files based on a given DB schema Model. Also accepts <path/model>

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

args.name: model
args.description: Model to scaffold. You can also use <path/model> to nest files by type at the given path directory (or directories). For example, redwood g scaffold admin/post

subcommands.name: sdl
subcommands.description: Generate a GraphQL schema and service object

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Also generate mutations

args.name: model
args.description: Model to generate the sdl for

subcommands.name: service
subcommands.description: Generate a service component

options.description: Overwrite existing files
Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript
Generate test files [default: true]
Generate Storybook files [default: true]

subcommands.name: types
subcommands.description: Generate types and supplementary code

subcommands.name: script
subcommands.description: Generate a script that can use your services/libs to execute with redwood exec script <name>

options.description: Generate TypeScript files Enabled by default if we detect your project is TypeScript

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute scripts generated by yarn redwood generate script to run one-off operations, long-running jobs, or utility scripts

args.name: script
args.description: Name of the script to execute

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build the api and web

options.description: Use Webpack Bundle Analyzer to visualize the size of Webpack output files via an interactive zoomable treemap
Print more information while building

subcommands.name: diagnostics
subcommands.description: Get structural diagnostics for a Redwood project (experimental)

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: Launch an interactive Redwood shell (experimental)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Print your system environment information

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade all @redwoodjs packages via an interactive CLI

options.description: Check for outdated packages without upgrading

Choices are "canary", "rc", or a specific version (e.g. "0.19.3"). WARNING: Unstable releases in the case of "canary" and "rc", which will force upgrade packages to the most recent release of the spec
Installs packages for the given PR

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: Runs a server that serves both the api and the web sides

options.description: What port should the server run on [default: 8911]
The socket the server should run. This takes precedence over port
The root path where your api functions are served
Forwards requests from the apiProxyPath (defined in redwood.toml) to the specified host

args.name: side
args.description: Which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: type-check
subcommands.description: Runs a TypeScript compiler check on both the api and the web sides

args.name: side
args.description: Which side(s) to check (Default to both)

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run Jest tests for api and web

options.description: Show help
Show version number
Run tests related to changed files based on hg/git (uncommitted files). Specify the name or path to a file to focus on a specific set of tests [default: true]
Run all tests
Show test coverage summary and output info to coverage directory in project root. See this directory for an .html coverage report
Delete the Jest cache directory and exit without running tests

Syncs the test database with your Prisma schema without requiring a migration. It creates a test database if it doesn't already exist [default: true]. This flag is ignored if your project doesn't have

args.name: sides or filter
args.description: Which side(s) to test, and/or a regular expression to match against your test files to filter by

subcommands.name: storybook
subcommands.description: Starts Storybook locally

options.description: Open Storybook in your browser on start
Build Storybook
Which port to run Storybook on (defaults to 7910)

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Lint your files

options.description: Try to fix linting errors

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Open your project in your browser

subcommands.name: setup
subcommands.description: Initialize project config and install packages

subcommands.name: auth
subcommands.description: Setup auth configuration for a provider

options.description: Overwrite existing files

subcommands.name: custom-web-index
subcommands.description: Setup an index.js file in web/src so you can customize how your Redwood App mounts to the DOM

options.description: Overwrite existing files

subcommands.name: generator
subcommands.description: SCopies a given generator's template files to your local app for customization. The next time you generate that type again, it will use your custom template instead of Redwood's default

options.description: Overwrite existing copied template files

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the generator template(s) to copy (see help for list)

subcommands.name: tsconfig
subcommands.description: Setup tsconfig.json on both web and api sides

options.description: Overwrite existing files

subcommands.description: Setup auth configuration for a provider

options.description: Database deployment for Render only  [default: "postgresql"]

options.description: Display this help message
Define the datasource provider to use

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generate artifacts (e.g. Prisma Client)

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Enable the Data Proxy in the Prisma Client
Hides the hint messages but still outputs errors and warnings
Generate a client for use with Accelerate only
Watch the Prisma schema and rerun after a change
Allow generating a client without models

Port to start Studio on
Browser to open Studio in
Hostname to bind the Express server to

options.description: Display this help message

subcommands.name: migrate

subcommands.name: dev
subcommands.description: The migrate dev command updates your database using migrations files during development

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Skip triggering seed
Skip triggering generators (e.g. Prisma Client)
Create a new migration but do not apply it. The migration will be empty if there are no changes in Prisma schema
The name of the migration. If no name is provided, the CLI will prompt you

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Reset your database and apply all migrations, all data will be lost

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Skip triggering seed
Skip triggering generators (e.g. Prisma Client)
Skip the confirmation prompt

subcommands.description: Apply pending migrations to update the database schema in production/staging

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.description: Resolve issues with database migrations in deployment databases

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Record a specific migration as applied
Record a specific migration as rolled back

subcommands.description: Check the status of your database migrations

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Compares the database schema from two arbitrary sources, and outputs the differences either as a human-readable summary (by default) or an executable script

options.description: Display this help message
A datasource url
A datasource url
Flag to assume from is an empty datamodel
Flag to assume to is an empty datamodel
Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff
Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datamodel' for the diff
Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff
Path to a Prisma schema file, uses the 'datasource url' for the diff
Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory
Path to the Prisma Migrate migrations directory
URL for the shadow database. Only required if using --from-migrations or --to-migrations
Render a SQL script to stdout instead of the default human readable summary (not supported on MongoDB)
Change the exit code behavior to signal if the diff is empty or not (Empty: 0, Error: 1, Not empty: 2). Default behavior is Success: 0, Error: 1.`

subcommands.name: db
subcommands.description: Manage your database schema and lifecycle (Preview)

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Ignore current Prisma schema file
Print the introspected Prisma schema to stdout
The datasource url
Specify the depth for introspecting composite types (e.g. Embedded Documents in MongoDB). Number, default is -1 for infinite depth, 0 = off

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: This command pushes the state of your Prisma schema file to the database without using migrations files

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
Skip generation of artifacts such as Prisma Client
Resets the database and then updates the schema - useful if you need to start from scratch due to unexecutable migrations
Ignore data loss warnings. This option is required if as a result of making the schema changes, data may be lost

subcommands.name: seed
subcommands.description: Seed your database

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema

subcommands.name: execute
subcommands.description: Execute native commands to your database

options.description: Display this help message
Custom path to your Prisma schema
The datasource url
Path to a file. The content will be sent as the script to be executed
Use the terminal standard input as the script to be executed
